original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults presenting to emergency care where domestic abuse is disclosed or clinically suspected; the text focuses primarily on women.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Emergency department recognition, confidential disclosure, injury documentation and local safeguarding support.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact only for confidential, clinician-led enquiry where suspicion exists, documentation at disclosure, injury documentation and recording child-welfare concerns. It is not used for universal screening or automatic police, shelter or referral action.

exact_sections.heading: Summary and Recognition of Domestic Abuse
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician suspicion, confidential disclosure opportunity and direct enquiry only when clinically indicated; it does not support universal screening.

exact_sections.heading: Disclosure, injury records, child welfare and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording disclosure, injuries, child-welfare concerns, and offered contact or support only when actually discussed or completed.

title: Management of Domestic Abuse
jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; requires UAE legal and safeguarding adaptation
population: Adults presenting to emergency care where domestic abuse is disclosed or clinically suspected; the text focuses primarily on women.
intended_setting: Emergency department recognition, confidential disclosure, injury documentation and local safeguarding support.
